content: Hi, I'm Dana, nice to meet you. I've been working as a wedding planner for about 10 years now, and I've just started my own agency in Los Angeles.
Nice to meet you too, Dana! Congratulations on starting your own agency, that's a big accomplishment. What made you decide to take the leap and start your own business?

I've always been passionate about weddings and events, and I felt like it was time to take control of my own career and create a business that truly reflects my vision. I've also been trying to find more time for my hobbies, like knitting and surfing.
Knitting and surfing are great hobbies, they're so different but both can be really relaxing. Do you have a favorite thing to knit, or a favorite surf spot?
I love knitting scarves and hats, and as for surfing, I've been going to Malibu a lot lately. I've also been trying to get into travel blogging, I love exploring new places and trying new foods.
Travel blogging is a great way to share your experiences with others, Dana. Have you thought about what kind of content you want to focus on, or what kind of tone you want to convey in your blog?

I've been thinking about that, and I think I want to focus on luxury travel and high-end events. I've also been thinking about starting an online course to teach others about wedding planning and event design.
An online course is a great idea, Dana! You could share your expertise and help others learn about the industry. What kind of topics do you think you'll cover in your course?
I've been thinking about covering topics like wedding design, vendor management, and timelines. I've also been thinking about including some case studies of my own weddings and events.

That sounds like a great plan, Dana. Including case studies will definitely make the course more engaging and relatable. Have you thought about how you'll market your course and reach potential students?
I've been thinking about using social media and email marketing to promote my course. I've also been thinking about partnering with other wedding professionals to help spread the word.
That's a great strategy, Dana. Partnering with other professionals in the industry can be a great way to build relationships and reach new audiences. What's your timeline for launching your course?
I'm hoping to launch my course by the end of the year, maybe in December. I've got a lot of work to do between now and then, but I'm excited to get started.
That's a great goal, Dana! You've got a clear plan and a lot of enthusiasm, I'm sure you'll do great. Do you have any other hobbies or interests that you're passionate about?
Actually, I've been trying to get into photography, I love capturing moments and beauty through my lens. Maybe I'll even start a photography business on the side.
Photography is a great hobby, and it could be a wonderful way to complement your wedding planning business. You could even offer photography services to your clients as an add-on.
